rationale: In the related-literature and theory-framing sections, Katona et al. (2011) is used for a specific mechanism, not only as a background list citation: the paper cites it to support the idea that tie strength can weaken as network size grows, affecting engagement. The citing paper reuses this logic when motivating why follower-count effects may differ across influencer tiers and campaign types. Although it does not reuse Katona et al.'s diffusion model directly, the citation materially shapes the conceptual setup and interpretation.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Katona et al. (2011) as conceptual groundwork on social-network structure and tie-strength effects, and uses that groundwork to frame hypotheses about nonlinear follower-size effects on impressions. It builds its influencer-selection argument on this prior network-based mechanism.
